title: Bacularia minor
intro: Bacularia minor, also known as Bupleurum, is a perennial plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly found in Asia and Europe, traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating liver and kidney conditions, and promoting overall immune system function. Bupleurum's bioactive constituents include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bupleurum is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs, such as glycyrrhiza and licorice root, to enhance its therapeutic effects.
